content:
Iron is a critical material in laser cleaning applications due to its prevalence in industrial components like structural steel or machinery parts. Its properties influence laser parameters for effective oxide or contaminant removal without surface damage. Below is a detailed table of iron's material properties relevant to laser cleaning processes. Note that laser cleaning of iron often targets rust (Fe2O3), requiring specific wavelength and energy settings to avoid excessive heat buildup, which can alter microstructure. Always conduct trials to optimize pulse duration and fluence for specific iron grades.

| Property                | Value                          |
|-------------------------|-------------------------------|
| Melting Point           | 1538°C                        |
| Thermal Conductivity    | 80.4 W/(m·K) at 25°C         |
| Specific Heat Capacity  | 0.45 J/(g·K)                 |
| Density                 | 7.87 g/cm³                   |
| Surface Reflectivity    | ~65% at 1064 nm (Nd:YAG laser wavelength) |
| Hard
